use_cases.name: SandboxLab Code Runner
use_cases.description: Multi-language code execution platform using CF Dynamic Workers (V8 isolates) for instant sandboxed code runs. Users paste code, pick language, execute in <5ms cold start. Supports JS, TS, Python (via Pyodide WASM). Per-execution billing via Stripe.
features: CF Dynamic Workers for isolate sandboxes

use_cases.name: AgentSmith Runtime
use_cases.description: AI agent execution platform where LLM-generated code runs in CF Dynamic Workers. Agents write code → sandbox executes it → results fed back to agent. Uses Workers AI for the LLM + Dynamic Workers for safe execution. Per-agent-run billing.
features: CF Dynamic Workers sandbox per agent step

use_cases.name: TenantScript Platform
use_cases.description: Multi-tenant SaaS platform where each customer runs custom business logic in CF Dynamic Workers. Customers upload JS/TS scripts that execute in isolated sandboxes. Built for no-code/low-code automation builders.
features: KV + D1 data access per tenant

use_cases.name: QueueFlow Orchestrator
use_cases.description: Visual workflow builder powered by CF Queues + Workflows. Drag-and-drop nodes (HTTP call, AI inference, transform, branch). Each step is a Queue message → Worker consumer. Durable execution with automatic retry.

use_cases.name: WebhookVault Pro
use_cases.description: Webhook ingestion and routing platform. Receives webhooks → CF Queue → fan-out to multiple destinations with retry. Guaranteed delivery, replay failed events, transformation middleware. For developers integrating third-party APIs.

use_cases.name: BatchForge AI
use_cases.description: Batch AI processing platform. Upload CSV/JSON → CF Queue fans out rows → Workers AI processes each (classify, summarize, extract). Results aggregate in R2. For data teams processing thousands of records through AI.

use_cases.name: EdgeChat AI
use_cases.description: AI chat platform running entirely on CF edge. Workers AI for inference (Llama 3.3, Mixtral), Durable Objects for conversation state, Vectorize for RAG. Zero external API dependencies. Sub-100ms responses globally.

use_cases.name: FluxCanvas Studio
use_cases.description: Image generation studio powered by FLUX.2 on Workers AI. Text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting. Gallery stored in R2. Credit-based billing. All inference on CF edge — no external GPU costs.

use_cases.name: VoiceEdge AI
use_cases.description: Voice AI platform using Deepgram Nova 3 (STT) + Workers AI (LLM) + Deepgram Aura 2 (TTS) on CF edge. Build voice assistants, podcast transcription, audiobook narration. All on CF infrastructure.
features: Deepgram Nova 3 STT via Workers AI

use_cases.name: RAGBuilder Pro
use_cases.description: No-code RAG pipeline builder using CF AI Search (AutoRAG). Point at R2 buckets or websites → automatic chunking, embedding, indexing. Query via API or built-in chat UI. For teams building knowledge bases.
features: CF AI Search for auto RAG pipeline

use_cases.name: CrawlHive Scraper
use_cases.description: Web scraping SaaS built on CF Browser Rendering /crawl API. Submit URLs → get structured data (HTML, Markdown, JSON). Scheduled crawls via Cron Triggers. Queue-based for bulk jobs. For data teams and AI training pipelines.
features: CF Queues for bulk crawl jobs

use_cases.name: ScreenSnap API
use_cases.description: Screenshot-as-a-Service API. Submit URL → get pixel-perfect screenshot or PDF. Powered by CF Browser Rendering. Supports custom viewports, full-page, element selection, dark mode. Developer API + dashboard.

use_cases.name: MCPHub Agent Store
use_cases.description: Marketplace for MCP-compatible AI agents built on CF Agents SDK. Developers publish agents, users subscribe and connect them to their AI tools. Each agent runs as a Durable Object with MCP server interface.

use_cases.name: TaskPilot Agent
use_cases.description: Persistent AI task agent built on CF Agents SDK. Assign tasks (research, monitor, report) → agent works autonomously using scheduled execution + hibernation. Wakes up, does work, sleeps. Email integration for reports.

use_cases.name: LiveBoard Collab
use_cases.description: Real-time collaborative whiteboard using Durable Objects for state + WebSockets for sync. Multiple users draw, add notes, vote in real-time. Each board is a Durable Object. Free tier + paid teams.

use_cases.name: EdgeDB Studio
use_cases.description: D1 database management studio. Connect to D1 databases, run SQL queries, browse tables, export data. Schema visualization. Like Adminer/pgAdmin but for CF D1. Free tool with pro features.

use_cases.name: MediaVault CDN
use_cases.description: Media management platform on R2. Upload, organize, transform, and serve images/videos with zero egress fees. On-the-fly image transforms via Workers. DAM for content teams.

use_cases.name: IcebergLake Analytics
use_cases.description: Data lakehouse on R2 using Apache Iceberg integration (R2 Data Catalog). Upload parquet/CSV → auto-create Iceberg tables → query via SQL. For data teams who want cheap analytics without Snowflake.

use_cases.name: ContainerDeploy Pro
use_cases.description: Container deployment platform on CF Containers. Push Docker images → deploy globally with scale-to-zero. Dashboard for managing deployments, logs, metrics. Simplified PaaS on Cloudflare.

use_cases.name: HyperQuery Proxy
use_cases.description: Database query accelerator using CF Hyperdrive. Connect existing Postgres/MySQL → get connection pooling + caching at the edge. Dashboard shows cache hit rates, query latency. For teams with existing databases.

use_cases.name: SandboxCI Pipeline
use_cases.description: CI/CD pipeline runner using CF Container Sandboxes. Push code → sandbox spins up Linux container → runs tests → reports results. Snapshot-based caching for fast rebuilds. For teams wanting edge-native CI.
features: CF Container Sandboxes for build env

use_cases.name: RealtimeChat Voice
use_cases.description: Voice-first AI chat using CF Realtime (WebRTC) + Workers AI. Users speak → Deepgram STT → Llama 3.3 responds → Deepgram TTS → audio back. Sub-second latency voice AI assistant.

use_cases.name: SemanticSearch Hub
use_cases.description: Semantic search engine builder on CF Vectorize + Workers AI embeddings. Upload documents → auto-embed with EmbeddingGemma → query with natural language. API + search UI. For SaaS products adding smart search.

use_cases.name: GatewayInsight AI
use_cases.description: AI cost management dashboard built on CF AI Gateway. Route all AI API calls through gateway → get unified analytics, caching, rate limiting across OpenAI/Anthropic/Google. For CTOs tracking AI spend.

use_cases.name: WorkflowForge ETL
use_cases.description: ETL pipeline builder using CF Workflows for durable execution. Extract from APIs/R2/databases → transform with Workers → load to D1/R2/external. Visual pipeline editor. Automatic retry on failures.

use_cases.name: NotebookEdge AI
use_cases.description: Jupyter-like notebook running on CF edge. Code cells execute in Dynamic Workers (JS/TS) or Container Sandboxes (Python). AI assistant via Workers AI explains code and suggests next cells. For data science education.

use_cases.name: FormBot Surveys
use_cases.description: AI-powered survey platform. Durable Objects track each respondent session. Workers AI generates follow-up questions based on answers. Real-time results dashboard via WebSockets. For product and UX teams.

use_cases.name: EdgeCron Monitor
use_cases.description: Website and API monitoring platform. CF Cron Triggers → Workers check endpoints from global edge locations → Queue failures for alerting → Workflows handle incident escalation. Uptime SaaS on CF stack.

use_cases.name: PluginSandbox Market
use_cases.description: Plugin marketplace where third-party developers upload extensions that run in Dynamic Worker sandboxes inside your SaaS app. Each plugin is isolated. For SaaS platforms wanting a safe extension ecosystem.

use_cases.name: DataPipe Ingest
use_cases.description: Real-time data ingestion platform. HTTP/webhook/MQTT sources → CF Queues → Workers transform → R2 (raw) + D1 (queryable) + Vectorize (searchable). For teams building data pipelines without Kafka/Kinesis.
